page: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.24xlarge is a high-performance computing server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor built on the x86_64 architecture, delivering 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs with a single thread per core. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, alongside cryptographic acceleration via AES and VAES.

The server is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 16.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on external storage options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 150 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the hpc7a.24xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, particularly in stress-ng multi-core tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and software compilation with Clang. Large-block memory bandwidth read and write operations also rank in the top tier, alongside Redis SET operations and PassMark physics simulations. This hardware profile is optimized for high-performance computing, parallel processing, scientific simulations, software compilation, and memory-intensive database workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.24xlarge is a high-performance computing server utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at 3.7 GHz with one thread per core. The system features 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, yielding 16.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. It delivers 150 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks place this server in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, large-block memory bandwidth, software compilation, and multi-threaded decompression. It is highly suited for compute-heavy parallel processing, scientific simulations, software compilation, and memory-intensive database workloads.

tagline: A dedicated 48-core high-performance computing server with 768 GB of DDR5 memory and 150 Gbps network bandwidth for parallel workloads.

768.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory providing 16.0 GB per core
